---
title: "HCC Research — Grants & Key Publications"
subtitle: "Texas 2009–2025"
format:
  html:
    theme: flatly
    embed-resources: true
    page-layout: full
filters:
  - timeline
---

```{=html}
<style>
  body { background: #f8fafc; font-family: 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; }
  h1.title    { color: #0f172a; font-size: 2rem; margin-bottom: .2rem; }
  p.subtitle  { color: #475569; font-size: 1.1rem; margin-top: 0; }

  /* ── Timeline global overrides ── */
  .timeline {
    --tl-color-line:   #cbd5e1;
    --tl-line-width:   4px;
    --tl-dot-size:     1.05rem;
    --tl-gap:          1.6rem;
    --tl-label-size:   0.85em;
    --tl-content-size: 0.9em;
    --tl-color-card-bg: #ffffff;
    padding: 1.5rem 0 2.5rem;
  }

  /* ── Card polish ── */
  .tl-card {
    box-shadow: 0 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,.08);
    border-radius: 10px !important;
    padding: 0.75rem 1rem !important;
  }

  /* ── Color legend ── */
  .legend {
    display: flex; gap: 1.4rem; flex-wrap: wrap;
    align-items: center; margin-bottom: 1.6rem;
    padding: .6rem 1rem; background: #fff;
    border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
    font-size: .82rem; color: #334155;
  }
  .legend-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; }
  .pip {
    width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 3px; flex-shrink: 0;
  }
</style>

<div class="legend">
  <strong style="color:#0f172a;margin-right:.3rem">Key:</strong>
  <div class="legend-item"><span class="pip" style="background:#1d4ed8"></span>CPRIT Grant</div>
  <div class="legend-item"><span class="pip" style="background:#b91c1c"></span>Publication (BCM / UTSW / MDA)</div>
  <div class="legend-item"><span class="pip" style="background:#6d28d9"></span>Ramirez Lab (UTHSCSA)</div>
  <div class="legend-item"><span class="pip" style="background:#047857"></span>Milestone</div>
</div>
```

::: {.timeline .tl-label-badge .tl-card}

::: {.event data-label="2009" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **CPRIT Established — First Grants Awarded**

*Statewide, Texas*

Voters approved Prop 15 authorizing **$3B** for cancer research. BCM and MD Anderson liver researchers were early recipients, launching Texas's HCC infrastructure.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2011" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **Hepatocellular Carcinoma** · El-Serag HB

*N Engl J Med 2011;365:1118–1127 · PMID 21992124*

Landmark global HCC review documenting the rapid rise of HCV-related HCC as the fastest-growing cause of US cancer deaths. BCM / Houston VA.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2012" style="--tl-color-accent: #6d28d9"}
🔬 **HCC Incidence & Risk Factors in TX Latinos** · Ramirez AG et al.

*PLoS One 2012;7(4):e35573 · PMID 22530052*

First study documenting South Texas Latinos have the nation's highest HCC rates (10.6/100K). Identified obesity and diabetes as key modifiable risk factors. UTHSCSA / IHPR.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2012" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **Epidemiology of Viral Hepatitis and HCC** · El-Serag HB

*Gastroenterology 2012;142(6):1264–1273 · PMID 22537432*

Quantified population-attributable fraction of HBV/HCV in HCC; foundational for Texas public health interventions. BCM.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2014" style="--tl-color-accent: #6d28d9"}
🔬 **HCC in TX Latinos 1995–2010: An Update** · Ramirez AG et al.

*PLoS One 2014;9(6):e99365 · PMID 24915432*

Confirmed TX Latino male/female HCC rates **3.1× and 4.0×** higher than non-Hispanic Whites. UTHSCSA / IHPR.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2015" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **RP150587 — Texas HCC Consortium (THCCC) ~$6M**

*BCM / UTSW / MD Anderson / UT San Antonio*

CPRIT Multi-Investigator Award launching the **THCCC** — 5,000-patient prospective cohort at 5 Texas centers for HCC risk factors and early detection biomarkers.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2017" style="--tl-color-accent: #6d28d9"}
🔬 **Lifestyle & Clinical Correlates of HCC — Aflatoxin** · Ramirez AG et al.

*Clin Gastroenterol Hepatol 2018;16(3):373–380 · PMID 28344065*

**First U.S. study linking aflatoxin exposure to HCC in a Latino-majority population.** Cases had 6× higher odds of detectable aflatoxins in blood. UTHSCSA / IHPR.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2017" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **AFP + Ultrasound for HCC Detection: Meta-Analysis** · Singal AG et al.

*Gastroenterology 2018;154(6):1706–1718 · PMID 29352041*

32 studies: AFP + ultrasound improves HCC sensitivity to 63% vs 45% for ultrasound alone. Basis for updated AASLD surveillance guidelines. UTSW.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2018" style="--tl-color-accent: #047857"}
🎯 **THCCC Cohort — 2,000+ Patients Enrolled**

*BCM / UTSW / MD Anderson / UT San Antonio*

Semi-annual ultrasound, AFP, labs, and biospecimens collected across 5 Texas sites. One of the largest prospective HCC biomarker repositories in the US.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2018" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **Epidemiology and Management of HCC** · Singal AG; El-Serag HB et al.

*Gastroenterology 2018;154(6):1693–1704*

Comprehensive review of HCC risk stratification, surveillance, staging, and treatment. Addressed NAFLD/NASH-HCC epidemic and rising rates in Hispanic populations. UTSW / BCM.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2019" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **TeCH Center CAP Round 1 — $13M**

*BCM / MD Anderson, Texas*

CPRIT Collaborative Award establishing the Texas Collaborative Center for HCC (TeCH), coordinating statewide HCC research.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2019" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **RP190641 El-Serag Biomarker Validation — $3M**

*BCM Houston*

Biomarker validation grant for HCC early detection research within the THCCC cohort.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2019" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **Hwang MDA Patient-Centered Prevention — $2.46M**

*MD Anderson Cancer Center*

Patient-centered liver cancer prevention initiative targeting at-risk populations.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2019" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **THCCC Cohort Study Design** · Loo N; El-Serag HB; Singal AG et al.

*Am J Gastroenterol 2019;114(3):530–532 · PMID 30699099*

Describes the THCCC 5,000-patient prospective cohort design and multi-center data collection protocol. BCM / UTSW / MDA / UTSA.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2019" style="--tl-color-accent: #6d28d9"}
🔬 **Metformin Modifies HCC Disparity in Diabetic Men** · Wang CP; Ramirez AG et al.

*Cancer Med 2019;8(6):3206–3215 · PMID 30993905*

In 84,433 men with T2D, metformin reduced HCC risk — stronger effects in Hispanic and African American men. UTHSCSA / VA.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2020" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **RP200537 Singal UTSW — $2.5M**

*UT Southwestern, Dallas*

3,000-patient cirrhosis cohort developing biomarker-based HCC surveillance algorithms.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2020" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **RP200554 Thrift BCM — $2.47M**

*BCM Houston*

Genetic epidemiology of HCC in Hispanics; focused on PNPLA3 and TM6SF2 variants.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2020" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **Epidemiology & Surveillance for HCC: New Trends** · Singal AG et al.

*J Hepatol 2020;72(2):250–261 · PMID 31954490*

Documents shift from viral hepatitis to NAFLD/NASH as dominant HCC etiology; projects **122% increase** in NAFLD-HCC by 2030. UTSW.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2020" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **AGA Update: HCC Surveillance in NAFLD** · Singal AG; El-Serag HB

*Gastroenterology 2020;158(7):1822–1830*

Joint AGA expert review on HCC screening in the rapidly growing NAFLD population. Addresses surveillance intervals and novel biomarkers. UTSW / BCM.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2022" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **PP220022 Community HCC Prevention — $1.96M**

*UT Health Texas*

Community-based HCC prevention at safety-net clinics targeting underserved Texas populations.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2022" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **RP220119 GALAD + AFP-L3 Validation — $2M**

*BCM Houston*

Validation of GALAD score and AFP-L3 as early detection biomarkers within the THCCC cohort.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2022" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **TeCH Center CAP Round 2 — ~$18M**

*BCM / MD Anderson / UTSW / UTHealth*

TeCH Center renewal expanding NASH-HCC research and health equity initiatives across Texas institutions.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2022" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **HCC Surveillance Improves Outcomes: Meta-Analysis** · Singal AG et al.

*J Hepatol 2022;77(1):128–139 · PMID 35139400*

59 studies / 145,396 patients: Surveillance yields 1.86× early-stage detection and **33% mortality reduction** (HR 0.67). UTSW.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2022" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **The Texas Collaborative Center for HCC (TeCH)** · Harrison AC; El-Serag HB et al.

*Front Oncol 2022 · PMID 36059708*

Describes TeCH's multi-institutional research, education, and advocacy framework targeting reduction of Texas liver cancer mortality by 2030.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2023" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **Lopez-Terrada TCH Pediatric HCC — $5.98M**

*Texas Children's Hospital*

Predictive biomarkers for pediatric HCC and hepatoblastoma.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2023" style="--tl-color-accent: #1d4ed8"}
🏆 **Zhong UTSW HCC Mechanisms — $1.05M**

*UT Southwestern, Dallas*

Investigation of molecular mechanisms driving HCC progression and therapeutic targets.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2023" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **Prevention of HCC — White Paper** · TeCH Consortium

*Hepatol Commun 2023 · PMC10524305*

Consensus recommendations covering HBV vaccination, HCV cure, metabolic intervention, and health equity strategies for Hispanic Texas communities.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2024" style="--tl-color-accent: #b91c1c"}
📄 **HES v2.0 Outperforms GALAD for HCC Detection** · El-Serag HB; Jin M et al.

*Hepatology 2024*

Phase 3 biomarker evaluation: HES v2.0 (AFP + AFP-L3 + DCP + clinical variables) demonstrated superior sensitivity and specificity over GALAD for early HCC detection. BCM / THCCC.
:::

::: {.event data-label="2025" style="--tl-color-accent: #047857"}
🎯 **CPRIT Surpasses $4B + UTSW NCI Liver Cancer SPORE**

*UTSW / BCM / MD Anderson, Texas*

CPRIT surpasses $4B total (~$59–77M to liver cancer). UT Southwestern receives the **first Texas NCI SPORE for Liver Cancer** with Singal, Yopp et al.
:::

:::
